---
title: "Exon-Attempt"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-11"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

```{r}
#Launch important packages
library(rtracklayer)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(rlist)
library(data.table)
library(rapportools)
```

```{r}
#Import the gtf file as a data frame with all columns better than the Kwok method
directory_in = "/Users/yahya/Desktop/STAR-aligner/GTF-FASTA/"
setwd(directory_in)
g <- readGFF("Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.115.gtf")
```

```{r}
data.table_gtf_protein_coding = g %>%
  dplyr::filter(gene_biotype == "protein_coding" ) %>% #keep protein-coding genes only
  dplyr::filter(type == "exon")%>% # keep exons only
  dplyr::filter(seqid != "MT") #Remove mitochondrial genes (to discuss?)
```

```{r}
n_skipped = 0
clock = 0

transcript_list = unique(data.table_gtf_protein_coding$transcript_id) #Collect all transcript names, to map each exon to its correct transcript 

#A loop to go through each transcript of the GTF
for (i in transcript_list) {
  clock = clock + 1 
  # Draft_table collects all the exons of a specific transcript (i-dependant) and ranks them from their starting codon
  draft_table <- data.table_gtf_protein_coding %>%
    dplyr::filter(transcript_id == i) %>%
    arrange(start)
  #For mono-exon genes
  if (nrow(draft_table) < 2) {
      Exon1 <- draft_table %>%
      dplyr::slice(1)
      if (!exists("dataframe_sj.edited")) {
      dataframe_sj.edited = Exon1}
    else { 
      dataframe_sj.edited = dataframe_sj.edited %>% 
      bind_rows(Exon1)} 
      next
  } 
  #Going through each exon in each transcript
  for (j in 1:(nrow(draft_table)-1)) {
    #Collect upstream exon information
    Exon1 <- draft_table %>%
      dplyr::slice(j)
    #Collect downstream exon information
    Exon2 <- draft_table %>%
      dplyr::slice((j+1))
    #Create a variable with the end of the upstream exon, and add one to have the coordinates of the first intronic base 
    END = Exon1 %>% pull(end)
    END = END + 1
    #Create a variable with the start of the downstream exon, and withdraw one to have the coordinates of the last intronic base
    START = Exon2 %>% pull(start)
    START = START - 1
    
    if (END > START) { #To avoid using overlapping exons
      n_skipped = n_skipped + 1 
      next}
    #Use Exon1 format to keep all information, simply by redifining it as an intron, and modifying the coordinates
    Exon1 <- Exon1 %>%
      dplyr::mutate(type = "intron") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(start = END) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(end = START)
    #Create a new dataframe containing all the introns
    if (!exists("dataframe_sj.edited")) {
      dataframe_sj.edited = Exon1}
    else { #Add the new intron to the dataframe
      dataframe_sj.edited = dataframe_sj.edited %>% 
      bind_rows(Exon1)}
  }
  #Print the clock
  print((clock/length(transcript_list))*100)
    }
```
